item.title: FirstFT: US accuses China of ‘industrial-scale’ theft of AI technology
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: PwC fined $166mn by Hong Kong regulators, and Meta to cut 10% of jobs

item.title: Griffin’s Citadel attacks Mamdani for using his penthouse to sell New York tax plan
item.description: Mayor called out billionaire’s $238mn property as he pushes for second-home levy on the wealthy

item.title: AI brings Foxconn a chance to cut its reliance on Apple
item.description: The cloud and networking division, which assembles AI servers, is growing at a pace the smartphone market cannot match

item.title: US shale bosses resist oil output boost amid Iran war ‘chaos’
item.description: Dallas Fed survey finds that energy executives are not confident high prices will hold

item.title: Intel shares jump 15% as it predicts revenue surge from AI data centres
item.description: US chipmaker reports better than expected revenue and financial guidance

item.title: Vanguard chief warns of ‘financial exploitation’ by prediction markets
item.description: Boss of $12tn asset manager echoes criticism of platforms such as Polymarket and Kalshi

item.title: Meta to cut 10% of jobs to ‘offset’ Zuckerberg’s AI spending
item.description: Social media group plans to spend $135bn on data centres this year

item.title: China hackers steal western secrets by targeting consumer gadgets
item.description: Compromised home internet routers and smart fridges are being used in cyber attacks, intelligence agencies warn

item.title: The welcome return of German military might
item.description: A defence strategy sets the right direction for dealing with the Russian threat

item.title: UK in talks with Anthropic over Mythos access for banks
item.description: British lenders seek advice from US groups testing powerful new cyber security AI model

item.title: Microsoft to offer 7% of staff voluntary redundancy for the first time
item.description: Long-serving employees given option for buyouts as it prepared to spend $140bn on AI investment this year

item.title: Man Group hit by single $6.1bn redemption
item.description: Withdrawal from one investor threatens to slow efforts to expand beyond hedge fund division

item.title: UK public sector borrowing hit £12.6bn in March as Iran war strains finances
item.description: Higher energy prices stoked by conflict have resulted in higher borrowing costs for government

item.title: KPMG to axe 10% of US audit partners
item.description: Cull comes after years of low uptake for voluntary retirement scheme

item.title: Warner Bros shareholders approve $111bn Paramount deal
item.description: Entertainment group’s investors delivered a protest vote against CEO David Zaslav’s $700mn pay package

item.title: Tesco and Sainsbury’s wisely stock up on market share
item.description: Both supermarkets want to avoid giving back any of their hard-won ground

item.title: White House accuses China of ‘industrial-scale’ theft of AI technology
item.description: Trump official Michael Kratsios says Chinese entities stealing from American labs

item.title: Thiel-backed start-up Stark expands into defensive drones
item.description: Fallout from war in Iran has fuelled demand for protection from UAVs

item.title: French weather service alerts police to tampering after suspicious Polymarket bets
item.description: Users of weather forum say Paris temperature data may have been manipulated for wagers on prediction market platform

item.title: US asset manager Voya Financial faces sale pressure from activist fund
item.description: Toms Capital Investment Management has built stake in $1.1tn in assets pension and insurance group

item.title: Arnault warns Middle East war could spiral into ‘global catastrophe’
item.description: LVMH’s billionaire boss says luxury group’s recovery hinges on the conflict being resolved quickly

item.title: BP suffers heavy defeat in investor climate vote
item.description: UK oil major lost two votes at its annual shareholder gathering over climate disclosures and electronic meetings

item.title: Trump says US navy will ‘shoot and kill’ any boat laying mines in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Oil rises as Tehran and Washington battle for control of waterway

item.title: What does AI really mean for your work? You asked, we answered
item.description: Sarah O’Connor, John Burn Murdoch and Madhumita Murgia replied to reader questions

item.title: Will AI widen inequality between workers?
item.description: This and more findings from our new poll of AI usage by thousands of US and UK workers

item.title: Almost half of EU banks have no women executives, watchdog finds
item.description: Eighth of firms surveyed have female chiefs, while women executives are paid 17% less than male peers

item.title: Medical data of 500,000 people in UK for sale on Chinese site
item.description: Alibaba ‘swiftly removed’ listings for UK Biobank data, institution says

item.title: How worried should Wall St be about the stablecoin threat to deposits?
item.description: The risk to US banks is real in theory but difficult to realise in practice

item.title: Blackstone pulls in $69bn despite slump in private capital returns
item.description: Asset manager drew investors even as performance in private credit and buyout units suffered

item.title: Anthropic and Freshfields agree deal to create legal AI tools
item.description: Tech group will tap ‘magic circle’ law firm’s expertise as it builds products that can be sold to rivals
